file_insights.changes: Added 293 lines of unit tests for cleanup logic
functions_added: setup_corrupt_card() - helper to create incomplete card structures
setup_valid_card() - helper to create valid card with full Meta
file_insights.testing_coverage: Comprehensive - 15 test cases covering scan and cleanup operations

patterns_discovered.description: setup_corrupt_card() and setup_valid_card() encapsulate directory and file creation, reducing duplication
Each test creates isolated temporary directory via tempdir().unwrap()
patterns_discovered.benefit: No test interdependencies; parallel test safety

patterns_discovered.description: Tests verify both presence detection (corrupt_cards.len() == 1) and file lifecycle (exists/!exists)
Separate tests for perform_cleanup(true) and perform_cleanup(false)

patterns_discovered.benefit: Verifies cleanup logic works across all card hierarchies

patterns_discovered.description: Test for both .bop and .jobcard extensions in final test

gotchas_discovered.gotcha: Timestamp-based age detection difficult to test
gotchas_discovered.issue: Cannot easily set filesystem timestamps in tests; comment explicitly notes limitation
gotchas_discovered.workaround: Test verifies logic path exists but acknowledges may not find old cards in practice; assertion uses len() == 0 || len() == 1

gotchas_discovered.issue: Full Meta initialization needed; requires specifying all optional fields even if unused
gotchas_discovered.mitigation: Centralized in helper function to avoid duplication across tests

gotchas_discovered.issue: Scan logic must skip both non-.bop directories AND regular files in state dirs
gotchas_discovered.evidence: Test explicitly creates both .join("not-a-card") and fs::write("file.txt")

gotchas_discovered.gotcha: Byte counting requires actual file writes
gotchas_discovered.issue: Assertion uses >= 1024 (not ==) due to potential filesystem overhead
gotchas_discovered.implication: Real-world byte reporting may include metadata

approach_details.strategy: Built comprehensive test suite using tempdir fixtures with isolated helper functions
test_categories: Scan detection: 6 tests (corrupt, old_failed, orphan_running, target_dirs, team_dirs, legacy formats)
Cleanup operations: 5 tests (dry-run, corruption removal, target removal, byte counting, orphan removal)
Edge cases: 4 tests (empty results, non-.bop dirs, .jobcard format, valid-only scenarios)
coverage_achieved: Happy path: valid cards remain untouched
Team namespaces: cleanup works across team-prefixed dirs
Safety: dry-run mode prevents premature deletion

recommendations.recommendation: Add timestamp manipulation utility for age-detection tests
recommendations.rationale: Current test for old_failed_cards is incomplete; could mock file modification time or add filetime crate
recommendations.implementation: Helper function to set file mtime via std::fs::set_file_times() or filetime crate

recommendations.recommendation: Document Meta field initialization burden in test helpers
recommendations.rationale: 25+ field assignments are verbose; helps future maintainers understand why full struct required
recommendations.implementation: Add comment explaining why all fields must be set (even with Default values)

recommendations.recommendation: Consider parameterized tests for multi-state scenarios
recommendations.rationale: Could use macro or external parameterized test crate to reduce duplicate test bodies
recommendations.implementation: If more state transitions added, refactor to use parameterized assertions

recommendations.recommendation: Add tests for concurrent cleanup scenarios
recommendations.rationale: Real usage may have multiple cleanup runs; test for TOCTOU race conditions
recommendations.implementation: Test when card is deleted between scan and cleanup, or file is open during cleanup

recommendations.recommendation: Document why byte assertion uses >= instead of ==
recommendations.rationale: Clarifies that filesystem metadata may add bytes beyond written data
